message:
So there I was at 11 PM, finally getting around to testing our smoke alarms, phone in one hand ready to look up *how* to test them properly... and of course the battery dies mid-search. Classic timing.

It made me realize I was basically winging it, which isn't exactly the vibe when it comes to fire safety. I ended up grabbing a step stool and just... hoping I was doing it right? Not ideal.

Turns out there's a real difference between a quick test and actually knowing your alarms are working the way they should—and yeah, having a plan that doesn't depend on your phone being charged at 3 AM.

Do you test your smoke alarms, or is that one of those things you keep meaning to get to? (No judgment—we've all been there!)
